results.raw_claim: A contraction mapping on a complete metric space has a unique fixed point.
A strictly concave function attains a maximum on a compact set.
A monotone sequence bounded above converges.

results.raw_claim: If one continuation-value function dominates another and β is nonnegative, Bellman operator monotonicity preserves that ranking state by state.
A constrained maximizer weakly dominates every feasible alternative in the objective ordering.
When a Bellman-style operator is contracting, the associated value function is its fixed point.
Convex preferences provide a convex utility representation on the full commodity space.
A contraction theorem on a complete metric space delivers a fixed point that can serve as an equilibrium object.
A continuous utility representation remains continuous when attention is restricted to a feasible subset.
A Bellman-style operator satisfying contraction conditions fits the contraction-mapping template used in recursive economics.
Complementary slackness forces a multiplier to vanish whenever the corresponding Kuhn-Tucker constraint is slack.
A witness profile satisfying the equilibrium predicate is enough to establish Nash equilibrium existence.
Policy improvement is reflexive for any evaluation criterion, providing a base case for policy-iteration arguments.
